name: Alto de Moncalvillo Loop
name_original: Dehesa de Hornos, Sotés – circular por Alto de Moncalvillo
description: This difficult 19.1-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Moncalvillo offers challenging climbs and technical terrain through forests and
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Hornos de Moncalvillo are situated in the western Iregua Valley, at the northern foothills of the Serradero Mountains within the Sierra de la Demanda. The landscape features diverse terrains, including wooded areas of pines and oaks, open heather, and agricultural land. Riders can expect varied topography with significant elevation changes, offering panoramic views of the Ebro Valley. Best mountain bike trails around Hornos De Moncalvillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) moderate route that takes about 3 hours 0 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain around the Valbornedo reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.2 km) path. This route features challenging ascents and descents through the Moncalvillo mountain area. Local mountain bikers also love the Alto de Moncalvillo Loop, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through the higher elevations of Moncalvillo, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Hornos de Moncalvillo is defined by wooded hills, agricultural valleys, and panoramic viewpoints, with routes catering to moderate and difficult skill levels. The region offers a network of mountain bike trails suitable for various physical conditions. The routes in Hornos de Moncalvillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hornos de Moncalvillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Hornos de Moncalvillo?
A: There are nearly 70 mountain bike trails around Hornos de Moncalvillo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Hornos de Moncalvillo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for mountain biking in Hornos de Moncalvillo due to the mild weather conditions. The region's diverse landscapes are particularly beautiful during these times.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 12 easy mountain bike trails in the area. While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted, these easier options provide a good starting point for less experienced riders. You can find routes with moderate difficulty like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655115/ventosa-dehesa-de-hornos-embalse-de-valbornedo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.4 km long.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes with significant elevation changes?
A: Hornos de Moncalvillo offers 17 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. For a challenging ride with significant ascents and descents, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655187/moncalvillo-btt-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 43.2 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993876776/alto-de-moncalvillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 30.8 km through higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655115/ventosa-dehesa-de-hornos-embalse-de-valbornedo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993876776/alto-de-moncalvillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Hornos de Moncalvillo?
A: The region offers diverse landscapes, including wooded areas of pines and oaks, open heather, and agricultural land, particularly vineyards. You can also enjoy panoramic views of the Ebro Valley and the surrounding Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Hornos de Moncalvillo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Hornos de Moncalvillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views, and the network of trails suitable for different physical conditions.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area boasts several natural and man-made landmarks. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4509944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also characterized by its extensive vineyards and forests.

Q: What other activities can I do in Hornos de Moncalvillo besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, Hornos de Moncalvillo is excellent for hiking, with routes like PR-25 leading towards Mount San Lorenzo. The area also features cultural attractions such as the 16th and 17th-century Parish Church of La Asunción and the Hermitage of El Cristo.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the region offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24320796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iregua Roman Road – Clavijo Castle loop from Entrena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 56.6 km trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655187/moncalvillo-btt-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop&lt;/a&gt; is also a substantial 43.2 km ride.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by water bodies?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655115/ventosa-dehesa-de-hornos-embalse-de-valbornedo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventosa, Dehesa de Hornos, Embalse de Valbornedo – circular&lt;/a&gt; traverses varied terrain around the Valbornedo reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Embalse de Valbornedo loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A place with a natural spring and native forest frequented by mountain bikers and walkers. The water is fresh and crystal clear. It is a magical and well-preserved place.

The highest point of the refrigerator route, where you can enjoy beautiful views. The antenna and the snow house are private, but if you get caught in the rain, you can take refuge in a hut in the area of the snowfields.

The Sierra de Moncalvillo is an authentic paradisiacal place. This mountain range that is part of the Iberian System has several peaks that exceed 1200m in height, such as Serradero, Neveras and Calva, from which a large part of the Ebro Valley can be seen, thus offering spectacular panoramic views.

Sotes is the classic Riojan crossroads of road and mountain bike routes. It is a welcoming town to make a stopover and among its buildings stands out the Renaissance church of San Martín from the 17th century. It is elevated above the Ebro valley so you can enjoy the views as you pass.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike route, demanding very good fitness and advanced riding skills. The trail includes challenging climbs and technical sections, typical of the Moncalvillo area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring a mix of 'mountain track route' and 'dirt roads that ascend'. You'll encounter narrow and technical trails, fast descents, and sections that can be rough or 'broken'. Expect varied ground, including rocky areas, especially in the wider Moncalvillo region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this 30.8-kilometer route takes approximately 3 hours and 27 minutes (12,430 seconds) for mountain biking. However, your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where does the Dehesa de Hornos mountain bike trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This circular route typically starts and ends in Hornos de Moncalvillo. While specific parking details aren't provided, Hornos de Moncalvillo is a common starting point for routes in the area, and you can usually find parking within the village.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in a landscape of lush pine and fir forests, green meadows, and oak and beech forests. The route offers beautiful views, including the View of the Moncalvillo Meadows, and passes through the scenic Alto de Moncalvillo.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or highlights on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, key highlights along the route include the Fountain of Life, the summit of Alto de Moncalvillo, and the View of the Moncalvillo Meadows. The route also passes through the settlement of Sotés.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and varied terrain, spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this route. The weather is typically milder, and the forests are vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring snow or icy conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits, fees, or specific regulations for mountain biking in the Sierra de Moncalvillo area?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Moncalvillo is a natural area of great scenic beauty. While there are no general entrance fees for accessing the trails, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or areas, especially during peak seasons or for organized events. Always respect local signage and environmental guidelines.

faqs.question: Is the Dehesa de Hornos mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Given the challenging nature of this route, ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers parts of the Neveras de Moncalvillo (22%), Ruta Dos Dehesas (22%), Dehesa del Prado (13%), and [CIMA LR09] Moncalvillo * Hornos de Moncalvillo (12%), among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A helmet is essential, and consider knee/elbow pads for technical sections. A GPS device or a downloaded map is highly recommended as cell service can be spotty.
